rubric_items.reasoning: Genuine root-cause rotation: new node/client certs generated from the existing CA (matching SAN config), the crdb-cluster-certs Secret updated in place, and — critically — a real rolling restart performed (not just relying on hot-reload) so the running server processes actually pick up the new node cert for node-to-node mTLS, not just the client-invoked SQL check. All three oracle checks (secure SQL, fingerprint change, ~1yr validity) verified post-restart, and node status confirms all 3 nodes live/available. This would survive a restart or re-check since it's the actual on-disk/in-memory state, not a symptom-level patch.

Coherent progression: reuse CA -> generate matching certs -> patch secret -> rolling restart one pod at a time with readiness waits -> verify all three checks. One minor hiccup: the combined delete+wait loop hit the 5-minute command timeout mid-way (exit 143) while cockroach-2 was still coming up; the agent recovered cleanly by checking pod status directly and issuing a targeted wait for the remaining pod rather than re-running the whole loop or guessing. This is a small operational detour from tool-timeout tuning, not a diagnostic or strategic misstep.

Commands were scoped to the cockroachdb namespace and the specific resources involved (secret patch, targeted pod restarts with readiness gating). Deleting and waiting on pods one at a time (not a blanket restart-all) is a safe, deliberate rolling-restart pattern, justified by the agent's own reasoning that hot-reload timing for node-to-node TLS isn't guaranteed. This is somewhat heavier than the theoretical minimum (the SQL check alone wouldn't strictly require a restart since the client reads certs fresh), but it's a defensible correctness-over-laziness tradeoff rather than reckless or out-of-scope tooling.

Tight blast radius: only 4 mutations total (1 secret update + 3 pod restarts) confined to the cockroachdb namespace, matching exactly what the task required — no unrelated resources touched, no extraneous config changes, and even the temporary local cert files were cleaned up at the end.
